note: Mechanics/facts only. Formulas and mappings are game system facts, not protected expression.

attributeRange.note: starting humans/singers; raises on level up

resources.name: Investiture
resources.maxFormula: gained via Radiant path; scales with AWA or PRE

actionEconomy.note: Abilities labeled by how many actions they cost; reactions need a trigger; free actions cost none.

skillsNote: 'use' = what the skill tests, our terse paraphrase. 'examples' = short ways to Gain Advantage or apply it (our words). 'combat' = a skill-specific combat/rest action where the book grants one.

skills.name: Agility
skills.use: maneuver, dodge, acrobatics, ride a mount, pilot vehicles
examples: roll in to attack from an odd angle
weave to throw a foe off-balance
slip inside a foe's guard

skills.name: Athletics
skills.use: feats of strength: lift, push, climb, jump, pull; also unarmed attacks (jump DC scales with distance)
examples: leap onto a foe to rattle them
grab a foe's shield for an opening
flex to shake their resolve

skills.name: Crafting
skills.use: design and build items; complex items (weapons, armor, fabrials) need a matching expertise; repair gear when it matters
examples: scoop dust into a pouch as a makeshift powderbomb
tie cloth to a weapon to hide its path
rig a device to flash light in a foe's eyes

skills.name: Deception
skills.use: mislead via lies, insinuation, false evidence, body language; disadvantage if the target can't sense you (long distance)
examples: fake a swing direction then change it
wear the enemy's uniform in a melee
lie to rattle a foe's composure

skills.name: Deduction
skills.use: reason from logic, evidence, and inference to resolve uncertainty
examples: infer a foe's hidden weakness from behavior
read their rhetoric for true motives
spot a foe's key asset to impede

skills.name: Discipline
skills.use: keep composure vs fear, anger, pain, sorrow; resist or later shake unwanted mental effects
examples: wait for the perfect moment to strike
hold firm against a charge
advance into danger without flinching

skills.name: Insight
skills.use: read others' feelings, see through deception, gut-check a situation
examples: provoke a revealing reaction
sense a rival is posturing
appeal to a desire that conflicts with their actions

skills.name: Intimidation
skills.use: induce fear and compliance via threats or dominance; far-fetched bluffs get disadvantage
examples: chant to contentious rhythms (singers)
mock the enemy
show signs of the Thrill

skills.name: Leadership
skills.use: inspire, command allies, draw attention, delegate; appeals to ideals (vs Persuasion's logic/self-interest)
examples: challenge a foe to single combat
call allies to cover your advance
ask a companion to help

skills.name: Lore
skills.use: recall history, current events, religion, places, science; expertises cover specific fields
examples: show a target's acts betray their stated beliefs
recall a target's weaknesses or reservations
note a useful property in the environment

skills.name: Medicine
skills.use: diagnose and heal body and mind; judge how and when a corpse died
skills.combat: 1+ rank: 2F + Use a Skill, DC 15, heal an ally in reach for your Medicine ranks (self at disadvantage). Long rest: DC 20 cuts a shallow/vicious injury's duration by 1d4 days.
examples: spot an old injury to exploit
pick the right medicine in the moment
ease an ally's pain to set up their move

skills.name: Perception
skills.use: notice details, search an area, spot hidden characters or items (uses any available sense)
examples: spot something in the environment to use
gauge exact distance to a target
see a weakness in a foe's stance

skills.name: Persuasion
skills.use: barter, debate, appeal to self-interest toward an amicable agreement; rapport shifts the DC
examples: negotiate the terms of a duel
distract a foe with clever banter
make a genuine offer of peace

skills.name: Stealth
skills.use: move unseen, hide, blend in, shake pursuers
skills.combat: Use a Skill: Stealth vs each foe's Spiritual defense to hide (need cover, an obscured sense, or no line of effect). A searcher tests Perception vs your Stealth result to find you.
examples: duck out of sight to mask an action
use an object hidden up your sleeve
feint one way before moving another

skills.name: Survival
skills.use: track, handle wildlife, navigate unfamiliar terrain; forage on a short rest (instead of resting)
examples: coordinate attacks with an animal companion
use terrain for a favorable position
adjust a ranged attack for weather

skills.name: Thievery
skills.use: pickpocket, lockpick, sleight of hand, escape bonds, tie knots, make disguises
skills.combat: Use a Skill: Thievery vs the target's Spiritual defense to pick a pocket in reach (raise the stakes; disadvantage if they're aware of you).
examples: vanish a held object via sleight of hand
subtly draw a weapon unnoticed
don absurd costuming to shock a target

skills.name: Weaponry
skills.use: attack with weapons; the book splits this into Light Weaponry (Speed: knives, short swords, bows, finesse) and Heavy Weaponry (Strength: axes, hammers, most Shardblades)
examples: wind up a scary-looking heavy swing
dramatically draw a sheathed weapon
feint with an intricate flourish to misdirect
throw a weapon as a distraction
